reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Phase 03 misunderstood the equivocation detection logic. The early return at L498-501 when head (slot, proposer) doesn't match incoming block is correct by design - the function specifically detects equivocation (same slot+proposer, different signatures). When slot/proposer don't match head, there's no equivocation to detect. The resubmitted block is correctly IGNORED (ValidationIgnore at L124) without further processing. Signature verification was already performed when the original block was first seen. The new block with mismatched head parameters is simply dropped, which is the correct security behavior. Spec reference: The consensus spec requires equivocation to be same slot+proposer; this implementation correctly follows that requirement.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: EIP-7951 SG-003 signature_verification requires signature checks on equivocation (same slot+proposer). Early return for mismatched head is spec-compliant.
